content: Curiosity-driven intrinsic reward mechanisms (from Topic A's ICM-based autonomous agents) can be repurposed to prioritize which new unlabeled experiences an active learning agent stores in its long-term memory, creating a self-organizing memory pruning system that outperforms 2024 SOTA fixed-importance retention rules. This framework lets the agent autonomously grow its memory store only with high-surprise, high-generalizability samples that directly improve downstream task performance, rather than relying on human-specified retention thresholds.
